search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG127 limb weakness recommendations adults
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
A weakness presentation does not establish stroke or a neuromuscular diagnosis.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults aged over 16 years with suspected neurological conditions; the legacy workflow has no age restriction.
setting_applicability: Direct for primary or community recognition and referral; emergency diagnosis and treatment remain outside the mapped content.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local emergency, imaging, and referral pathways.
recency_verification: The current official NG127 adult recommendations, last updated 2023-10-02, were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG127 remains the current official NICE suspected-neurological-conditions guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult weakness identity, onset, distribution, progression, fluctuation, sensory, speech, pain, acute or rapidly progressive red flags, bowel or bladder context, imaging, safety, and referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness, urgent referral, and clinician-led imaging where specified.

unresolved_source_gaps: People aged 16 years or younger are outside the exact source.

Vision and functional-impact fields, all broad assessed negatives, detailed neurological examination prompts, sensory-level and headache-with-deficit wording, blood tests, prior notes, generic plans, and fixed or generic follow-up remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, imaging selection or interpretation, emergency disposition, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
